title: lepidium × bupleurifolium
intro: Lepidium × bupleurifolium, also known as garden cress or land cress, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, resulting from the cross between Lepidium sativum and Bupleurum rotundifolium. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lepidium × bupleurifolium include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Lepidium × bupleurifolium can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or capsule, and is often consumed as a fresh or cooked vegetable, offering a rich source of vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients.
